# Liferay Design, repository guide for LLMs

Project: liferay-design/liferay.design
Repo: https://github.com/liferay-design/liferay.design
Purpose: Legacy Gatsby site for Liferay Design, including editorial content, team pages, events, newsletters, resources, handbook content, and historical yearly pages.

## Critical compatibility constraints
- Treat this as a legacy stack.
- Use Node 12.x for local work. The repo README explicitly says it only works with Node v12, recommends Node v12.14.1, and Netlify is pinned to NODE_VERSION="12".
- Gatsby is pinned to v2.32.13 and React to v16.14.0.
- The project still depends on node-sass 4.14.1 and other Gatsby 2-era plugins, so do not assume modern Node, modern Sass, or Gatsby 4/5 conventions are safe.
- Prefer minimal, surgical changes over framework upgrades.

## Start-up and local development
- Install dependencies with npm install.
- Start dev with npm run dev.
- The dev script first runs src/utils/generateEnv.js, then launches Gatsby on host 0.0.0.0 and port 7777, opening a browser automatically.
- Local URL is intended to be http://0.0.0.0:7777 and is also reachable on the LAN.

## Secrets and external data
- The repo expects a .env.development file for API keys and other secrets.
- generateEnv.js auto-creates a placeholder .env.development file if it does not exist.
- README warns that the site may not build fully without API keys, and some sections may be missing.
- Mailchimp-backed newsletter data is special-cased in gatsby-node.js: if MAILCHIMP_KEY is missing, Gatsby schema stubs are created so newsletter queries resolve to an empty collection instead of crashing.
- The dependency list also indicates integrations or data dependencies around Firebase, Google APIs, Mailchimp, Netlify CMS, Algolia DocSearch, and a custom plugin called plugins/gatsby-source-sheets.

## High-level structure
- src/markdown contains most of the site content, plus YAML data such as Authors.yaml, Changelog.yaml, Countries.yaml, and Offices.yaml.
- src/pages contains page entry points and historical sections, including yearly directories 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, plus pages like index.js, team.js, articles.js, changelog.js, careers.js, tags.js, alumni.js, lexicon.js, and lexicon-1.js.
- src/components follows atomic design structure: atoms, molecules, organisms, templates.
- src/components/templates contains template folders such as Articles, Blueprints, Careers, Events, Handbook, Lexicon, Lexicon-1, Newsletters, Resources, Tags, Team, MainLayout, and PrivatePage.
- src/utils contains repo-specific scripts and helpers like generateEnv.js, generateIcons.js, logRocket.js, typography.js, and generateTracks.
- static stores public assets like admin, files, images, video content, and redirects.

## Routing and page generation
- MDX pages get a slug field from Gatsby's createFilePath, with /markdown/ stripped out.
- Template selection is convention-based: the first path segment after the leading slash is capitalized and mapped to src/components/templates/<Template>/index.js.
- Tag pages are generated at /tags/<kebab-case-tag>/.
- Newsletter archive pages are generated at /newsletter/YYYY-MM.
- There is an attempt to configure client-only routes for gated sections like blueprints and handbook in gatsby-node.js. Be careful when editing this area because it is legacy code and may already contain brittle logic.

## Content model assumptions
- Most editorial work should happen under src/markdown, not by hardcoding content into React pages.
- New MDX content should match an existing template slug unless you are intentionally adding a new template and updating the generation flow.
- YAML files in src/markdown likely act as supporting datasets for authors, changelog items, offices, and countries.

## Styling and theming
- Global styles are loaded from theme/global.scss via gatsby-browser.js.
- Typography is configured in src/utils/typography.js.
- A custom Theme UI folder exists at src/gatsby-plugin-theme-ui.
- Do not introduce modern styling assumptions without checking the existing SCSS and Gatsby 2 stack first.

## Icons and assets
- SVG icons in static/images/icons are transformed into a React icon map by src/utils/generateIcons.js.
- The generated output is written to src/components/atoms/Icon/icons.js.
- If icons change, keep the generation workflow intact instead of hand-editing generated icon output.

## Code style and tooling
- JavaScript-first codebase with TypeScript config used mainly for path aliases and allowJs, not for TS emission.
- tsconfig.json defines path aliases for components/*, images/*, and theme/*.
- ESLint uses babel-eslint, react rules, prettier, and liferay/react config.
- Preserve the existing module style and project conventions when possible.

## Guidance for AI assistants
- Do not casually upgrade Gatsby, React, Node, node-sass, or major plugins as part of a small task.
- Assume backward compatibility is more important than modernization unless the task is explicitly a migration.
- Prefer content changes in src/markdown and small component fixes over architectural refactors.
- Check whether a page is driven by MDX + template before editing a page component directly.
- Be cautious with build-time data integrations, because missing keys may make parts of the site appear broken locally.
